notes: NI Studio Drummer — multi-mic acoustic drum kit in Kontakt. Three kits included: The Fab (vintage Gretsch character), The Beast (modern rock/metal Ludwig), The Classic (all-round studio kit). All kits share the same CC layout. Quick Controls map to the per-kit mixer channels — kick, snare, toms, hi-hat, overhead, and room — giving direct level control without opening the Kontakt UI. CC 11 (Expression) rides the master output. CC 64 can toggle hi-hat open/closed on some patches. For individual drum compression and transient shaping, map those parameters via right-click → Learn MIDI CC inside Kontakt. General MIDI note mapping applies: kick = C1 (36), snare = D1 (38), hi-hat closed = F#1 (42), open = A#1 (46), crash = C#2 (49), ride = D#2 (51) — check the instrument's mapping view for full layout.

Expression.description: Expression — master output level. Use to automate the drum kit level in a mix.

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — hi-hat open/closed toggle on some patches

Quick Control 1.description: Kick Level — combined kick in + kick out mic blend level in the kit mixer

Quick Control 2.description: Snare Level — combined snare top + snare bottom level in the kit mixer

Quick Control 3.description: Hi-Hat Level — hi-hat close mic level

Quick Control 4.description: Tom Level — floor and rack tom mic blend level

Quick Control 5.description: Overhead Level — stereo overhead pair; natural kit spread and cymbal detail

Quick Control 6.description: Room Level — room microphones; adds size, depth, and ambient glue to the kit

Quick Control 7.description: Parallel Compression Amount — built-in parallel bus compression; adds punch and density without killing dynamics

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random velocity and timing variation between hits for a more played-in, less quantised feel

Reverb.description: Reverb send level — adds extra ambience beyond the built-in room mics

Brightness.description: Tone brightness — high-shelf across the kit bus
